title: Regularisation, counterterms, and continuum limit of $F_{\rm TECT}$
statement: The TECT functional admits a consistent regularisation with a finite counterterm set rendering physical quantities cutoff-independent, and the continuum (lattice-spacing $\to 0$) limit exists with scheme-independent physics.
scope: OPEN scaffold. Counterterm closure and renormalisation-scheme independence (ROADMAP Stage-1, GOVERNANCE sec-2 Sector-A scope). Distinct from A1 (the kernel convention), which fixes the bare normalisation, not the UV structure.
falsifier: A physical observable retaining cutoff dependence after the declared counterterm set, or a new divergence requiring a non-renormalisable counterterm.
no_overclaim: No renormalisability or continuum-limit theorem is established; this registers the requirement.
next_action: Enumerate the divergence structure of the BCC lattice functional and the minimal counterterm set; state the continuum-limit criterion.
